Duplicate calendar entry (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiffs Demand For Production Of Documents And Things And Defendants' Appearance At Depositions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declartion Of Laura L. Campbell; Separte Statement
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laura L. Campbell of Goldstein of Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP, Telephone (415) 673-5600, appearing for Plaintiff Polk Street Group LLC;
Sean B. Gentry of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP, Telephone (415)795-3579, appearing for Defendants Bitters Bock & Rye Inc., et al;

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications as follows:

PLAINTIFF POLK STREET GROUP LLC'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiffs Demand For Production Of Documents And Things And Defendants' Appearance At Depositions

Motion granted in part. Defendant License to Spill Inc. dba Blur shall within 10 days of this order serve verified responses and documents to RPD Nos. 1-40, and 48. Objections not based on privilege are overruled. If privilege is claimed as to any documents, defendant shall prepare a privilege log stating at a minimum, title and type of document, date created, preparer, recipient, general subject matter, and legal reason for withholding.

Motion against License to Spill Inc. dba Blur denied as to RPD No. 41, 42, 43, 44, (overbroad, fails to identify documents sought with reasonable particularity) 45-46 (overbroad, potentially invades privacy in employment records) and 47 (0verbroad, fails to identify documents sought with reasonable particularity)

Defendant Bitters, Bock & Rye Inc. (BB&R) shall within 10 days of this order serve verified responses and documents to RPD Nos. 1-32, 47, and 49. Objections not based on privilege are overruled. If privilege is claimed as to any documents, defendant shall prepare a privilege log stating at a minimum, title and type of document, date created, preparer, recipient, general subject matter, and legal reason for withholding.

Motion against BB&R denied as to RPD No. 33-44 (overbroad, fails to identify documents sought with reasonable particularity) 45, 46, 48 (overbroad, potentially invades privacy in employment records) and 47 (Overbroad, fails to identify documents sought with reasonable particularity)

Motion denied as to defendant Ronald Kirkpatrick. The motion is premature as it was filed before a response had been served and there was no opportunity for anyone to meet and confer.

Sanctions request by moving party denied. Moving party failed to comply with notice requirements of CCP sec. 2023.040.

Sanctions request by defendants denied.

Quickbook data to be provided by 4:00 p.m. on Friday September 7, 2018. All other Responses due by September 14, 2018.

Plaintiff's counsel is to submit/email order approved as to form and that complies with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem Charles Geerhart at cgeerhart@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Continued To Sep-24-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laura L. Campbell, of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP, Tel. 415-673-5600, for plaintiffs;
Sean B. Gentry, of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP, Tel. 415-795-3579, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF POLK STREET GROUP LLC Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses is denied. While the 45-day limitation for filing a motion to compel after service of verified response for filing is not jurisdictional, it renders the court without authority to rule on motions to compel other than to deny them. (Sexton v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County, 58 Cal. App. 4th 1403.) The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief For Untimely Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laura L. Campbell, of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP, Tel. 415-673-5600, for plaintiffs;
Sean B. Gentry, of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP, Tel. 415-795-3579, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF POLK STREET GROUP LLC request for C.C.P. 473 relief is not available for failure to file a document request discovery motion within the 45 days mandated by C.C.P. 2031.310. Weil and Brown Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1150, citing Zellerino v. Brown (1991) 235 Cal. App. 3d 1097, does suggest relief may be obtained under C.C.P. Section 473. However the Court of Appeal held in that case C.C.P. 473(b) relief can only be obtained when the Discovery Act does not provide a remedy. Zellerino involved expert disclosure and none of the cases citing Zellerino involved the 45 day rule. There has been no reported case where C.C.P. 473 relief was granted when the 45 day rule applied. The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
